hallucinations.description: All academic citations in the paper are placeholders such as '**?**', '**??**', or '**??**' (e.g., in the abstract, introduction, related work, and method sections). These do not correspond to any real papers and appear to be fabricated or hallucinated references to prior work on model collapse.
hallucinations.evidence: Examples include: '**?** first characterized model collapse as a “curse of recursion”...', '**?** formalized self-consuming loops...', and similar patterns throughout the Related Work and Introduction sections.

hallucinations.description: Reported experimental metrics (e.g., specific KL divergence, perplexity, and tail coverage values for TADA vs. baselines) do not consistently match the actual code outputs or ablation summaries, which show TADA often underperforming random/centroid anchors on distributional metrics and tail coverage being saturated at 1.0 across conditions.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper Table 1 and Figure descriptions claim TADA achieves best tail coverage (1.00) with mixed KL/perplexity results, but code summaries (e.g., ablation_summary.json and research_summary.json) show non-monotonic KL, saturated tail coverage, and cases where TADA has the highest KL divergence.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found, primarily nonexistent citations and inconsistencies in reported experimental results that do not align with the provided code outputs. The severity is moderate to high due to fabricated references and potentially invented quantitative findings.
